name: Biconomy Account Abstraction API
what_it_does: Account abstraction infrastructure for Web3 applications — providing Bundler API (ERC-4337 UserOperations), Paymaster API (gasless transactions), and Smart Account SDK for enabling user-friendly blockchain interactions without requiring users to hold native gas tokens.
use_cases: Enabling gasless transactions where the dApp sponsors gas fees for users via Paymaster API
Bundling multiple blockchain operations into single atomic user operation
Building Web3 agents that submit transactions without managing native gas tokens
Creating smart wallet accounts with custom validation logic via Smart Account SDK
Onboarding Web2 users to Web3 without requiring ETH for gas fees
not_for: Simple read-only blockchain queries (use Alchemy or Infura directly)
Chains not supported by Biconomy infrastructure (verify chain support first)
Applications where users must control gas payment (gasless is the primary value)
best_when: An agent needs to sponsor gas fees for users, batch transactions, or create smart account wallets following ERC-4337 account abstraction standard.
avoid_when: You only need read access to blockchain data or your use case doesn't benefit from account abstraction features.

auth.notes: API key from Biconomy dashboard. Separate keys for Bundler and Paymaster services. Dashboard manages gas tank funding for Paymaster. Key passed in request headers.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 10,000 UserOperations/month; Paymaster requires funding gas tank
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-UserOperation; gas tank deposit covers sponsored transactions
pricing.notes: Bundler API free tier available. Paymaster requires depositing ETH/stablecoins into gas tank for sponsorship. Usage-based pricing on UserOps volume. Supports 15+ EVM chains.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; higher on paid plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ERC-4337 standard errors plus Biconomy-specific error codes. SDK simplifies UserOperation construction. Gas estimation available before submission. Dashboard shows transaction history and gas usage.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations idempotent. UserOperation submission creates new on-chain transactions. Same UserOperation hash is idempotent if not yet mined.
known_agent_gotchas: Gas tank must be funded before Paymaster can sponsor transactions — operational prerequisite
ERC-4337 UserOperation lifecycle is async — submit, wait for bundling, then mine
Not all EVM chains supported — verify chain compatibility before building
Gas estimation can fail for complex transactions — build retry logic with adjusted gas limits

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. Smart contracts audited — check audit reports on website. Gas tank management requires careful access control. Open-source SDK for client-side operations.
